_meta.description: Cross-provider instance family taxonomy. Prefixes are matched case-insensitively against the provider's native instance-type or plan-name strings. Hand-seeded from the v0 snapshot set; AWS, GCP, Azure, IBM, Oracle, Vultr, Linode all covered. See SPEC.md for the schema and AGENTS.md for how the agent surfaces the dimensions_matched / dimensions_not_normalized fields.

schema.members[].prefix: Provider-native instance-type or plan-name prefix. Matched case-insensitively. Anchored at the start of the identifier.
schema.members[].notes: Free-text architecture / generation notes for agent prose.
schema.dimensions_matched: Dimensions the cross-provider equivalence holds on.
schema.dimensions_not_normalized: Dimensions the equivalence does NOT hold on. The agent must surface these in prose.

members.notes: M-series = balanced Intel/AMD/Graviton x86 + ARM. T-series = burstable. a1 = first-gen Graviton ARM. Source attribute: instanceFamilyCategory == 'General Purpose'.
N-series = balanced Intel (n2 = Cascade Lake, n4 = Emerald Rapids). N2D/N4D = AMD EPYC. E2 = cost-optimized burstable. T2A = Ampere ARM, T2D = AMD Milan ARM-replacement scale-out.
D-series = balanced (Dsv5/Dasv5/Dasv6 = Intel Ice Lake / AMD Milan / Genoa). B = burstable. A = legacy basic tier. DS = D-series with premium storage attached. Source attribute: armSkuName prefix.

bx = balanced VPC virtual server. bx3d adds local NVMe instance storage. bx4 = 4th gen Intel Sapphire Rapids. bxf = balanced + FPGA-accelerated generation. Plan-name suffix '-NxN' encodes vCPU x RAM_GB.

Standard shapes. E-series = AMD EPYC (E5 = Genoa, E6 = Turin). A-series = Ampere ARM (A1 = Altra, A2/A4 = AmpereOne). X-series = Intel (X9 = Ice Lake, X12 = Granite Rapids). E3/E4/E5/E6/A1/A2/A4/X9/X12 are Flex shapes with separate per-OCPU and per-GB-RAM parts; X5/X7 are fixed-shape single-OCPU.

vc2 = Cloud Compute (shared CPU, Intel). vhf = High Frequency (3GHz+ Intel). vhp = High Performance (AMD or Intel dedicated cores). voc-g = Optimized Cloud general-purpose. Distinguished by the plan id `type` field plus voc subprefix.

Linode `class` field: nanode (single shared vCPU), standard (shared), dedicated/premium (dedicated vCPU). g7-dedicated and g8-dedicated are newer dedicated-CPU generations alongside g6-dedicated. All map to general-purpose for v0.
C-series. Suffix letters: a = AMD, g = Graviton ARM, i = Intel, d = NVMe local storage, n = enhanced networking. Source attribute: instanceFamilyCategory == 'Compute Optimized'.
C-series = compute-optimized (C2 = Cascade Lake, C3 = Sapphire Rapids, C4 = Emerald Rapids). C2D/C3D/C4D = AMD. C4A = Google Axion ARM. H3/H4D = HPC-optimized compute.
F = compute-optimized (Fsv2 = Intel Cascade Lake, Falsv6 = AMD). FX = high-frequency Intel for EDA. H/HB/HC/HX = HPC-class (HBv4 = AMD EPYC Genoa with InfiniBand).

cx = compute-optimized VPC. cx4 = 4th gen Intel Sapphire Rapids. cxf = compute-on-FPGA-host (preview tier in some regions). nxf = network-optimized variant (high-network-bandwidth workloads); v0 buckets this with compute-optimized for cross-provider comparison since IBM is the only provider in v0 with a distinct network-optimized line.

Oracle does not bucket compute-optimized as a top-level shape line. 'Optimized' shapes (X9-Optimized) and HPC bare-metal serve this role. HPC nodes ship with cluster networking. Listed VM members are partial; verify against snapshot.
Optimized Cloud Compute, compute-tier variant. Plan id pattern voc-c-<vcpu>c-<ram>gb-<disk>s-<vendor>. Always dedicated cores.

Linode does not publish a separate compute-optimized class. Dedicated CPU plans (g6-dedicated, g7-premium) are the closest analogue; surface that caveat when the user asks for compute-optimized on Linode.

R-series = 1:8 vCPU:GB. X-series = 1:16+ ratio (in-memory DB / SAP HANA). U-series = up to 32 TB RAM (u-3tb1/u-6tb1/u-9tb1/u-12tb1 = older Nitro generation, u7i/u7in = current). Z1D = high-frequency memory-optimized. Source attribute: instanceFamilyCategory == 'Memory Optimized'.
M-series = SAP HANA / in-memory analytics. M3/M4 = newer Intel generations with persistent memory options.

E-series = 1:8 memory-optimized (Esv5 = Ice Lake, Easv5 = Milan, Eadsv7 = Genoa). EC = confidential compute. M-series = very large RAM (up to 11.4 TB). G/GS = legacy memory-optimized (deprecated for new deployments but still priced).
mx = memory-optimized VPC (1:8 ratio). mz2 = large-memory SAP-certified variant. Plan-name suffix '-NxN' encodes vCPU x RAM_GB; ratios will exceed 1:4.

Oracle does NOT have a dedicated memory-optimized shape line. Modern Flex shapes (E5/E6/A2/A4/X9/X12) let the user pick the OCPU:RAM ratio at provision time (typically up to 1:16). Surface that the equivalence-by-family does not apply: pick a Flex shape and set memory >= the requested GB explicitly.
Optimized Cloud Compute, memory-tier variant. Plan id pattern voc-m-<vcpu>c-<ram>gb-<disk>s-<vendor>. 1:8 ratio.
Linode `class` == 'highmem'. 1:30 vCPU:GB ratio (very memory-heavy).
I-series = high-IOPS NVMe local SSD. i4g/im4gn/is4gen = Graviton-2 storage variants. D-series = dense HDD storage (warehousing). Source attribute: instanceFamilyCategory == 'Storage Optimized'.
Z3 = storage-optimized with local SSD attached. GCP's only storage-optimized family in v0.
L-series = storage-optimized with local NVMe (Lsv2 = AMD EPYC, Lsv3 = Intel Ice Lake, Laosv4 = AMD).
vx = very-high-storage VPC. vx3d adds local NVMe.
DenseIO shapes price OCPU, Memory, AND NVMe-TB separately (three SKU lines). E5 DenseIO is the current-gen storage-optimized line.
voc-s = Optimized Cloud storage-tier variant. Plan id pattern voc-s-<vcpu>c-<ram>gb-<disk>s-<vendor>. Distinct from voc-c/voc-m/voc-g which are compute/memory/general-purpose.
Linode does not publish a storage-optimized class. Disk size scales with the standard plans; surface that caveat.

G-series = graphics/inference (NVIDIA T4/A10G/L4/L40S). P-series = training (V100/A100/H100/H200). F1/F2 = FPGA. inf1/inf2 = AWS Inferentia ML inference. trn1/trn1n/trn2 = AWS Trainium ML training. All grouped under gpu/accelerated for v0. Source attribute: instanceFamilyCategory == 'Accelerated Computing'.
A-series = NVIDIA A100 (a2) / H100 (a3) / B200 (a4). G2/G4 = NVIDIA L4 / L40 graphics. TPU SKUs are separate and not in v0 GPU family.
NC = compute GPU (V100/A100/H100). ND = training (A100/H100). NV = visualization (A10/T4/V710). NG = AMD MI200/Radeon. NP = FPGA. NM = AI accelerator. NCC = confidential GPU.
gx3 = GPU VPC (NVIDIA L40S / H100). gx3d adds local NVMe. Bare-metal GPU lives under is.bare-metal-server, not is.instance.
Oracle GPU SKUs price the GPU itself per-hour (one part per GPU model). The compute envelope (OCPU + Memory) is included in the GPU SKU price. Power Systems (power-iaas) is out of v0.
vcg = Cloud GPU. Plan id pattern vcg-<gpu-model>-<vcpu>c-<ram>g-<vramGB>vram. Supports NVIDIA A16, A40, A100, L40S.
Linode `class` == 'gpu' or 'accelerated'. gpu = NVIDIA RTX 6000 / RTX 4000. accelerated = Netint VPU (video codec acceleration).

AWS bare-metal shapes share parent family prefixes (e.g. m7i.metal-48xl, c7i.metal-48xl). productFamily == 'Compute Instance (bare metal)' in the snapshot identifies them. The agent must filter by productFamily, not by prefix, for AWS bare-metal queries.
GCP does not publish bare-metal compute in the standard Compute Engine catalog. Bare Metal Solution is a separate product not fetched in v0. Surface the absence.
Azure does not publish standalone bare-metal VMs in the Retail Prices API for v0. Azure Dedicated Host and Azure VMware Solution exist as separate products. Surface the absence.
These are bare-metal-server plans (service is.bare-metal-server, NOT is.instance). Same family letters as VPC but on dedicated hardware. ux = ultra-memory (up to multi-TB).
BM = Bare Metal. Oracle prices these as separate part numbers under serviceCategory == 'Compute - Bare Metal' or 'Compute - GPU'.
Vultr Bare Metal is a separate product line and not in the /v2/plans response. Surface the absence.
Linode does not publish bare-metal VMs in /v4/linode/types. Surface the absence.
